rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+). Does the student leave the chair while the agent works?
One exercise = one bounded activity.
Pattern-recognition LOs need n≥2 instances with contrast.
Student agency — adults decide. Forced phases, enforced pauses, yoga-instructor pacing are paternalistic.
Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens.
Artifact-realism check. Every named tool, skill, connector, file, or path must exist and be available.
Teach the principle and the technique — default. Students must leave knowing WHY it works and HOW to reproduce it.
Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go — forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
Corporate infra is nice-to-have, never a forcing function.
Measure silent failure, not just friction.
Cohort-delivered exercises run on trainer-paced concurrent execution, not sequential.
Host-skill is optional; default cohort is no host-skill.
Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write.
Classroom is default delivery; body teaches work, trainer manages room. No conversations authored in body.
Offered structures need a forced-engagement mechanic — defaults and plans alike get rubber-stamped.
When task selection drives the learning outcome, name both floor AND ceiling explicitly.
Audience tier determines authoring substrate.
Symmetric visible-mechanics flow > runtime fork when dual-runtime constraints break the agentic move.
Load-bearing platform feature gets no fallback — gate or redesign.
Plug points are integration commitments, not labels; UX — operating ease for student in seat.
Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: maintainer block (line 182–199): no leap-test section is present. The maintainer block contains Meta, Quality, Strategy reference, Eval instance, and pre-cohort TODOs, but zero 'Leap test' entry naming three observable Monday-morning outcomes the student exhibits on their own.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a 'Leap test' entry in the maintainer block naming three specific, falsifiable Monday-morning actions: e.g. 'runs a solo Claude interview before the next team meeting', 'names the crux of a real rollout problem unprompted', 'steers Claude away from a tidy diagnosis back to the group's real constraint'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function names its dominant failure and one recovery move.
rules_evaluated.evidence: maintainer block: no per-phase failure mode + escape hatch rows. Phase 1 forcing function ('push back once') and Phase 2 forcing function ('steer heavily if needed; make Claude really see') are named in body prose but the maintainer block has no row enumerating dominant failure modes (e.g., driver accepting a tidy crux, group not reading the synthesis before approving) and no documented escape hatches (trainer move, fallback prompt, Nerd line) for each phase.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add failure-mode + escape-hatch rows for each phase's forcing function: e.g. Phase 1 — dominant failure: participant agrees with first push-back, escape hatch: trainer asks 'is that a symptom or the cause?'; Phase 2 — dominant failure: group accepts Claude's tidy crux without pushback, escape hatch: prompt 'list the other cruxes' or trainer names the dry-run 'no time to experiment' example.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Heavy fan-out prompts get a body-side recovery callout.
Convenience-driven prompts with known shape-bias get body-side acknowledgment callouts.
Session widget — **Session** *(verb, "name")* source shape; place by learning flow.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 18: `**Prompt** *(Cowork)*` — Phase 1 first prompt uses the runtime label as the widget header. There is no session-open widget (e.g. `**Session** *(open, "find-the-crux")*`) in the exercise. The exercise-top position should carry the session widget per canonical pattern (a).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a session widget at exercise top (before Phase 1) in the form `**Session** *(open, "find-the-crux")*`; the runtime label inside each prompt block stays as-is.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff.
Multi-session (or any new mechanic mid-arc) ships as packaging, not as a new taught primitive.
/clear is a tool with a purpose, not a ceremony.
